author: terencechain
content: prysm drops payload attestation if its received before the block

author: nflaig
content: that can explain why they are not in the block, but attesters should still observe them

author: potuz
content:
so 7218 was not even seen by Prysm. So from the point of view of Prysm it had to build on empty.  The vote there is not enough to allow Prysm to reorg, you need to have a threshold of not available, and that payload from the point of view of the ptc attestations in teh block definitely has them. Now, I assume that attesters did see the payload (except prysm) and had a different PTC view. So indeed, why was the next block built on top of Prysm's?

Could be a grandine-erigon bug that just constructs 7220. That block includes two attestations:

author: potuz
nflaig
content: ah I just checked, our fork choice didn't accept 7219 as head 🙂

author: potuz
content: and you see that there's weight for both, some attesters are attesting for 7219 and some for 7218 full, so the view was split at that slot in fact

content: yeah it's messed up, lodestar would have reorged the block and build on 7218 full
